findings.message: PII (owner_email) is logged directly via log.info(). The model explicitly marks owner_email as 'PII: owner email address — do not include in logs or external responses' (models.py:16), but this is violated by logging it on line 47.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from the log.info() call. If audit purposes require tracking item creation, log only non-PII fields like item_id, creation timestamp, or item_name.

findings.message: Unnecessary PII collection: owner_email is accepted in ItemCreate schema but never used for the stated purpose (item creation). The create_item() call on line 45 only uses payload.name. Collection of PII without clear purpose violates data minimization principles.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from the ItemCreate schema unless there is a legitimate business reason to store it. If auditing requires capturing user identity, use a session context or authentication metadata instead of accepting it as input.
